research.product: Microsoft Graph user delta query (Microsoft Entra users change tracking)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, GET /users/delta (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0)

official_sources.title: user: delta - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/delta request shape; Authorization: Bearer required header; $deltatoken/$skiptoken state-token parameters; $select supported with id always returned; $expand/$top/$orderby unsupported; query parameters specified once in the initial request and encoded into @odata.nextLink/@odata.deltaLink which must be copied verbatim; 200 OK user collection responses

official_sources.title: Use delta query to track changes in Microsoft Graph data
official_sources.used_for: nextLink-until-deltaLink round structure (a page never contains both); opaque state tokens; @removed tombstone shape with reasons changed/deleted appearing in any page; replay tolerance; synchronization reset contract: 410 Gone with a Location header holding a full-resync URL with an empty $deltatoken; directory delta token 7-day validity

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header in seconds and TooManyRequests error envelope; recommended handling: wait the Retry-After delay, retry the same request, repeat while throttled

research.language_rationale: The synchronizer runs inside an existing .NET identity-provisioning service, and the pinned integration surface is Microsoft Graph's documented REST delta protocol; C# with HttpClient and System.Text.Json exercises the raw v1.0 contract (opaque links, tombstones, Retry-After, 410 resync) without hiding it behind the Graph SDK's DeltaRequestBuilder.

verified_facts: The delta function lives at GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/delta and every call requires an Authorization: Bearer header.
$select is the supported projection parameter; the id property is returned even if not selected; $expand, $top and $orderby are not supported for users/delta.

Responses carry either @odata.nextLink (more pages this round, $skiptoken) or @odata.deltaLink (round complete, $deltatoken) but never both; state tokens are opaque and the whole link URL must be reused unmodified.

Removed users arrive as { id, "@removed": { "reason": "changed" | "deleted" } }; changed means soft-deleted/restorable, deleted means permanent; tombstones may appear in any page including the initial enumeration.
Replays are documented: the same entity can appear multiple times in one round and clients must merge by id.
Throttling returns 429 with a Retry-After header in whole seconds and an error envelope with code TooManyRequests; the documented recovery is to wait Retry-After seconds and repeat the request.

A synchronization reset returns 410 Gone with a Location header containing a fresh full-sync URL (delta path with an empty $deltatoken); the application must restart a full synchronization from that URL.
Directory delta tokens expire after seven days, so long-lived synchronizers must handle the reset path as routine.
